steps.title: Distribute the subtraction
steps.content:
Subtract every term inside the parentheses. The minus sign changes the signs of $8x^6$, $6x$, and $-5$:

$$6x^3-4x+7-(8x^6+6x-5)$$
$$=6x^3-4x+7-8x^6-6x+5$$

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content:
Group terms with the same powers:

$$-8x^6+6x^3+(-4x-6x)+(7+5)$$
$$=-8x^6+6x^3-10x+12$$

So the equivalent expression must have an $x^6$ term of $-8x^6$, an $x^3$ term of $6x^3$, an $x$ term of $-10x$, and a constant term of $12$. The answer is B.
